dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.8 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Right-click any account in the Standard or Tiny popover for the full action menu — Connect / Open / Refresh / Disconnect web usage, Rename, Switch, Force switch, Remove. Previously these actions were only reachable from the Full layout's row context menu and the ellipsis button. Web-linked accounts now report the same percentage shown on claude.ai. The scraper used to read a raw `aria-valuenow` value that did not match the displayed "X% used" text (we observed 14% in the widget for a bar the page rendered as 7%, and 52% for 59%); it now parses the visible percentage from the bar's labelling cell and only falls back to a normalised aria reading if no text label is present.
Fixed web-linked accounts showing percentages that did not match what claude.ai's usage page displayed for the same window.

The menu-bar popover still dismisses when the Rename window opens (macOS menu-bar app behaviour, not preventable without giving up the dropdown style). The rename itself goes through on Save; reopen the menu to see the new name. The web-usage scraper now reads the visible "X% used" text in the labelling cell. If a future claude.ai markup change moves that text far from the progressbar element, the widget falls back to a normalised `aria-valuenow / aria-valuemax` reading — accurate when both attributes are present, but worth re-checking against the live page if numbers drift again. Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` scope shipped in 11.3. The cb_gsheets_* tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
